fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 68 incorrectly checks `not clamp` instead of `clamp`. This inverts the logic, causing the result to be clamped when clamping is disabled, and not clamped when it is enabled.

spec.expr: y__1 >= min(y0, y1) and y__1 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: The bug only manifests when the interpolated value falls outside the calibrated y-range, which is when the clamping logic is needed.

spec.expr: (not clamp and y__4 == y__1) or (clamp and y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: The value of y after the conditional block must correctly reflect whether clamping was applied.
All passing tests operate within the interpolation range (ratio in [0,1]), while all failing tests involve extrapolation.
